evals.prompt: I have a PostgreSQL function calculate_order_total(order_id integer) that returns numeric. It should sum line_items.price * line_items.quantity for the order, and return 0 if the order has no items. Write me a pgTAP test file for it following TDD.
evals.expected_output: A complete .sql test file using BEGIN/SELECT plan(N)/SELECT * FROM finish()/ROLLBACK structure with sections for existence (has_function, function_returns), happy path (is/results_eq), edge case (zero items returns 0), and at least one error/NULL behavior assertion. Test must be wrapped in a transaction and rolled back.
expectations: The skill SKILL.md was read
The output is a complete .sql file with a BEGIN ... ROLLBACK transaction
SELECT plan(N) appears with N matching the actual count of test assertions
SELECT * FROM finish() appears before ROLLBACK
has_function() and function_returns() are used to verify structure
is() or results_eq() is used to verify the happy path returns the correct sum
An assertion checks the zero-items edge case returns 0
NULL handling for the order_id argument is addressed (either via is() with NULL or noted in a comment)

evals.prompt: We have an audit_log trigger called trg_users_audit on the users table. It should fire AFTER UPDATE and insert a row into audit_log capturing the old and new email. It should NOT fire on INSERT or DELETE. Write a pgTAP test that verifies this behavior.
evals.expected_output: A .sql file that verifies the trigger exists (has_trigger / trigger_is), then exercises the trigger by performing an UPDATE and asserting audit_log received exactly one row with correct old/new values, plus negative-case assertions confirming INSERT and DELETE do NOT produce audit_log rows.
expectations: The skill SKILL.md was read
object-recipes.md was read for the trigger recipe
has_trigger() verifies the trigger is attached to the users table
trigger_is() verifies the trigger calls the expected function
An UPDATE is performed and an assertion (results_eq, is, or row_eq) verifies the audit_log entry
A negative test confirms INSERT does not produce audit_log rows (is_empty or similar)
A negative test confirms DELETE does not produce audit_log rows
The test runs inside BEGIN ... ROLLBACK

evals.prompt: I have a posts table with an RLS policy posts_owner_select that should let authenticated users read only their own rows (where user_id = current_setting('app.user_id')::int). Help me write a pgTAP test that proves this policy works — including that other users can't see the post.
evals.expected_output: A .sql test file using SET LOCAL ROLE (or SET LOCAL app.user_id) to switch identities, with assertions for: (a) the policy exists (policies_are or similar), (b) the row owner can SELECT their row, (c) a different user gets is_empty when selecting the same row, (d) SECURITY note about superusers bypassing RLS.
expectations: The skill SKILL.md was read
advanced-patterns.md was read for the RLS pattern
policies_are() or has_policy/policy_cmd_is is used to verify the policy exists
SET LOCAL is used to change identity within the test transaction
An is_empty() (or set_eq with empty result) assertion verifies the OTHER user cannot see the row
An assertion verifies the OWNER can see their own row
The test acknowledges that superusers bypass RLS (via comment, NOTICE, or by not running as superuser)
The test runs inside BEGIN ... ROLLBACK
